title: 10 best herbal creams for allergies

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol, which helps to reduce swelling and itching associated with allergic reactions, providing relief from skin irritation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold, such as flavonoids, help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate allergy symptoms, protecting the skin from damage and promoting healing.

Immunomodulatory effects: Pot marigold has been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing the overactive immune response that can contribute to allergic reactions, helping to alleviate symptoms like itching, redness, and inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with allergic reactions. Calendula's natural moisturizing properties also help to hydrate and protect the skin, promoting a healthy barrier against allergens and other external irritants. This makes Calendula creams a popular natural remedy for alleviating allergy symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate allergic reactions by inhibiting the release of histamine, a key player in allergic responses.

Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the symptoms of allergic reactions such as itching, redness, and swelling.

Carotenoids (including Beta-Carotene): As antioxidants, carotenoids help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to allergic reactions and inflammation, promoting overall skin health and resilience.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm allergic skin reactions.
Leaves: They are used because of their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them beneficial for soothing allergic skin irritations.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce swelling and combat infections caused by allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Combine dried calendula flowers with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Stir the mixture constantly for 5 minutes until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and let it cool and set completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions.
Antioxidants: Aloe vera contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, thereby reducing the severity of allergic responses.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's gel-like texture provides a soothing and moisturizing effect on the skin, helping to calm irritated skin caused by allergic reactions and reduce redness and itching.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with allergies because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cooling and moisturizing effects of aloe vera help to reduce redness and swelling associated with allergic reactions. Additionally, aloe vera has natural antihistamine properties that can help to neutralize histamine, a chemical released by the body during allergic reactions, thereby providing relief from itchy, scratchy, and inflamed skin caused by allergies. This makes aloe vera an effective natural remedy for allergy sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: Glucomannans are a type of polysaccharide that help alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system's response to allergens.
Saponins: Saponins present in Aloe vera have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe skin irritations and reduce the severity of allergic reactions.

Vitamins and Polyphenols (specifically C and E): The presence of vitamins C and E, along with other polyphenols, acts as antioxidants in the skin, neutralizing free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress that can exacerbate allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are most commonly used due to their high content of gel, which contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties beneficial for allergy relief.
Gel (from Leaves): The gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera is widely used due to its rich moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate allergy symptoms.

Aloe vera Leaf Juice (from Leaves): Aloe vera leaf juice is also commonly used due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which provide soothing and calming effects for allergy-prone skin.
Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are most commonly used due to their high content of gel, which contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties beneficial for allergy relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 leaves of aloe vera and carefully peel off the outer skin to expose the gel inside.
Weigh out 1 cup of aloe vera gel and transfer it to a blender with 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Blend the mixture on high speed for 5 minutes then add in 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 30 minutes then whip it with a hand mixer until it thickens.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile creams contain apigenin, an flavonoid that reduces inflammation and soothes itchy skin, providing relief from allergic reactions such as eczema and hives.

Antihistamine properties: Chamomile contains bisabolol, a terpene that blocks the action of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions, which can help alleviate symptoms like itching, redness, and swelling.

Sedative properties: The apigenin in chamomile also has a sedative effect on the body, which can help calm the immune system and reduce the severity of allergic reactions, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with allergies because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm irritated skin. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have potent anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and swelling associated with allergic reactions. These properties also help to reduce itching and discomfort, providing quick relief from allergic skin irritations. Additionally, chamomile creams can help to stabilize the skin's natural barrier function, reducing the risk of future allergic reactions.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, reducing the severity of allergic reactions and inflammation in the body.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-itching properties, which help alleviate symptoms of allergic skin reactions such as itching and redness.

Matricaria chamomilla terpenoids: These terpenoids, particularly alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, exhibit anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relieve respiratory symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as congestion and coughing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used because they contain flavonoids and essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing allergic reactions.
Leaves: They are used because they contain similar compounds to the flowers, providing a similar soothing effect for allergic skin irritations.
Seeds: They are used because they contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, helping to alleviate allergic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 100ml of cold-pressed oil for 2-3 weeks to create chamomilla infused oil.
Combine 10g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter and 20ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 30ml of chamomilla infused oil to the beeswax mixture and heat until the mixture reaches 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 120°f to 140°f before whipping it.
Pour the whipped cream mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Echinacea, the main active compound in Kansas coneflower, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness associated with allergic reactions.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower cream help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate allergy symptoms, such as itchiness and irritation, by reducing oxidative stress.
Immunomodulatory: Kansas coneflower's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune system's response to allergens, preventing overactive reactions and promoting a balanced immune response.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate congestion. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, inhibit the release of histamine, a key player in allergic reactions. As a result, Echinacea angustifolia creams can provide relief from itchy skin, runny nose, and sneezing associated with seasonal allergies, making it a natural and effective remedy for those seeking alternative solutions.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside and echinulin, help alleviate allergy symptoms by modulating the immune system, reducing inflammation, and inhibiting the release of histamine.

Polyacetylenes: These compounds, especially echinacin, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.

Phenolic acids: Specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat allergy symptoms by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are used due to their high concentration of phenolic compounds and polysaccharides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm skin irritations.
Flowers: Echinacea angustifolia flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms and reduce itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when they are at their highest potency for 10-15 minutes.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Grind 30 grams of dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of powdered echinacea angustifolia with 5 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler for 5 minutes.
Whip the mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency and pour it into a container for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as itching, redness, and swelling.
Antioxidants: Herbal ginger creams are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C, E, and beta-carotene, which neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress that can exacerbate allergic responses.

Natural antihistamines: Ginger has been shown to possess natural antihistamine properties, helping to alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with allergies by blocking the action of histamine.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce histamine release. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which contribute to allergic reactions. Additionally, ginger creams have been found to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can further reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected areas, providing relief from allergy symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of allergic reactions by soothing the skin and preventing the release of histamine.

Shogaols: Shogaols, such as 6-shogaol, have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system and alleviate allergic symptoms by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing the severity of allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and itching, making them a popular ingredient in creams for allergies.
Buds: Buds are used for their potential to help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with allergies.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50 grams of fresh zingiber officinale roots in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain 450 milliliters of oil.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the filtered oil with 100 milliliters of distilled water and 20 milliliters of glycerin.
Emulsify the oil and water mixture with the melted wax and butter mixture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's curcuminoids reduce inflammation, which helps alleviate symptoms such as itching, swelling, and redness associated with allergic reactions.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental allergens.
Anti-allergic properties: Turmeric's flavonoids and polyphenols have been shown to inhibit histamine release, reducing allergic responses such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with allergies because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric cream has the ability to modulate the immune system, reducing the release of histamine and other allergic mediators. This helps to alleviate symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling associated with allergic reactions. Additionally, turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from allergic skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps alleviate allergies by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which in turn suppresses the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound found in Curcuma longa, helps with allergies by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing the severity of allergic reactions.

Voluric acid: Voluric acid, a sesquiterpene, helps alleviate allergies by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of Curcuma longa is used to make creams for allergies due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used to make creams for allergies due to their ability to reduce inflammation and itching.
Seeds: The seeds of Curcuma longa are used to make creams for allergies due to their potential to inhibit histamine release, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of curcuma longa powder to obtain the desired herbal extract.
Combine 500 grams of curcuma longa powder with 1 liter of water in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 40 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to enhance curcuma longa solubility.
Strain the mixture using a coffee filter to remove curcuma longa particles from the liquid.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 200 grams of curcuma longa extract to create a stable cream base.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lavender cream's antihistamine properties help to reduce allergic reactions by blocking histamine, a chemical released during an allergic response, from binding to its receptors, thereby reducing symptoms such as itching, swelling, and redness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy-related symptoms like swelling, redness, and itching by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Calming and sedative effects: The calming and sedative properties of lavender cream can help to soothe itchy skin and reduce anxiety, stress, and discomfort associated with allergies, promoting relaxation and reducing the overall severity of allergic reactions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The key compound linalool in lavender oil has been shown to reduce the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergy symptoms. The cream's soothing and calming effects also help to reduce itchiness, redness, and swelling associated with allergic reactions, providing relief and comfort to those affected. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to traditional treatments for allergy sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps with allergies by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, thereby alleviating symptoms such as itching and redness.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that helps with allergies by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce histamine release and alleviate allergic reactions.

Luteolin: A flavonoid that helps with allergies by inhibiting the release of histamine from mast cells, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and modulating the immune response to alleviate allergic symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Flowers: They are used due to their calming and soothing effects, which help reduce stress and inflammation caused by allergies.
Seeds: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the skin and reduce allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 lb of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers to ensure high quality and potency of the final product.
Infuse 8 oz of carrier oil with 1/2 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the oil mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of shea butter and 8 oz of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to a spreadable consistency at room temperature in 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and itching associated with allergic reactions, providing relief from discomfort and skin irritation.

Antihistamine-like effects: The menthol in peppermint oil has natural antihistamine-like effects, which can help to neutralize histamine released during an allergic reaction, reducing symptoms such as itchiness and hives.
Cooling and soothing properties: Peppermint cream's cooling sensation helps to calm irritated skin, providing quick relief from itching, redness, and discomfort associated with allergies.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect, which can help reduce swelling and itching associated with allergic reactions. The herb also contains compounds like limonene and beta-pinene that can help alleviate congestion and sinus pressure, providing relief from allergy symptoms. Additionally, peppermint's ability to thin mucus can make it easier to expel, further alleviating congestion and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: This phenolic compound helps reduce inflammation and has anti-histamine properties, making it effective in alleviating allergy symptoms.
Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene, caryophyllene oxide has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Menthone: A monoterpene, menthone has decongestant properties that help to relieve nasal congestion and other respiratory issues associated with allergies.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Stems: Stems of Mentha x piperita are also used to make creams for allergies, as they contain similar compounds to the leaves, offering relief from allergic reactions.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Mentha x piperita are used to make creams due to their rich content of menthol, which helps to soothe and calm the skin affected by allergies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves early in the morning on a sunny day.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a rotary evaporator at 40°c for 2 hours.
Combine 20g of the distilled mentha x piperita essential oil with 80g of emulsifying wax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over a double boiler at 70°c while stirring for 30 minutes with a spatula.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and let it set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Omega-6 fatty acids: Borage cream contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce allergic reactions and alleviate symptoms such as itchiness, redness, and swelling.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in borage cream, including flavonoids and terpenes, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the severity of allergy symptoms like itching, sneezing, and congestion.

Soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds: Borage cream contains a range of bioactive compounds, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm irritated skin and mucous membranes, reducing the risk of allergic reactions and promoting overall skin health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borago officinalis, also known as borage, creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream is rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a polyunsaturated fatty acid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies such as itchiness, redness, and swelling. Additionally, the cream contains antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to allergic reactions. This makes borage cream a popular natural remedy for soothing and calming irritated skin.
remedy_constituents: Squalene: This triterpene helps with allergies by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to allergic reactions.

Polyphenolic compounds (including flavonoids and phenolic acids): These compounds help with allergies by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the severity of allergic reactions and promote tissue repair.

Glycosides (including inulin): These compounds help with allergies by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune system, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can alleviate allergic symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties to soothe and calm allergic reactions.
Seeds: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of allergies.
Flowers: Known for their antihistamine properties and ability to reduce itching and redness associated with allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal potency.
Combine the harvested herb in a saucepan with 250ml of jojoba oil and heat at 40-60c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl to separate the oil from the solids.
Add 50g of beeswax to the oil mixture and heat at 60-80c for 10 minutes stirring constantly.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and let it set for 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help to reduce swelling and itching caused by allergic reactions, providing relief from itchy skin and redness.

Antihistamine properties: Sage has natural antihistamine properties, which can help to block the release of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions that causes symptoms like itching, sneezing, and congestion.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in sage creams can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, further reducing the severity of allergic reactions and promoting skin health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains compounds like camphor and thujone, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease congestion. Additionally, sage has been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs, associated with allergies. The cream's soothing and calming effects may also help to reduce redness and itching, providing relief for those suffering from allergic reactions. This makes Salvia officinalis creams a popular natural remedy for allergy sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help reduce inflammation and alleviate allergic symptoms by binding to proteins and preventing them from triggering allergic reactions.
Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate allergic responses and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies.

Caffeic acid (a phenolic compound): Caffeic acid has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's response to allergens and alleviate allergic symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain bioactive compounds such as tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm allergic reactions.

Flowers: The flowers have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in allergic conditions.

Seeds: The seeds are rich in essential oils, particularly thujone and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can help to alleviate allergic symptoms such as itching and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 days.
Infuse 20g of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 100ml of carrier oil such as olive oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Whip the strained oil with a hand mixer or a whisk to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with allergies because they contain soothing and calming properties that alleviate itchiness, redness, and swelling associated with allergic reactions. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds in herbal elder creams help to reduce histamine release, thereby minimizing allergic symptoms. Additionally, the natural ingredients in these creams work to moisturize and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by allergies. As a result, herbal elder creams provide effective relief for itchy, irritated skin and can be a gentle and natural solution for managing allergy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as redness and itching.

Antihistamine and antipruritic properties: Elder creams contain natural antihistamines and antipruritics that help block the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions, and soothe itching, scratching, and other discomforts associated with allergies.

Vasodilatory effects: The anthocyanins and flavonoids in elder cream also have vasodilatory properties, which can help dilate blood vessels and reduce swelling, further alleviating symptoms of allergic reactions like hives, rashes, or redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them an effective ingredient in creams for allergy relief.
Barks: The bark of the elder plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to good agricultural practices, ensuring proper handling and storage.
Combine one part dried sambucus nigra flowers and one part dried sambucus nigra leaves in a glass container, weighing 50 grams total.
Steam distill the mixture using 500 milliliters of water for 30 minutes, resulting in a fragrant essential oil rich in bioactive compounds.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of shea butter, warming gently until fully incorporated and smooth.
Blend the sambucus nigra essential oil with the beeswax mixture, adding 1% by weight of vitamin e oil as a preservative, achieving a homogenous cream consistency after 15 minutes of mixing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle creams helps with allergies because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb Hibiscus sabdariffa, also known as roselle, contains bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies. The cream's topical application allows for direct absorption into the skin, targeting affected areas and providing rapid relief from itching, redness, and swelling caused by allergic reactions. Additionally, roselle creams may help modulate the immune system, reducing the body's overactive response to allergens and promoting a state of balance and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Roselle cream contains histamine-binding sites, which can bind to histamine receptors in the body, reducing the allergic response and providing relief from symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, help reduce inflammation caused by allergies, alleviating conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis.

Antioxidant properties: Roselle cream's antioxidant activity helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate allergic reactions and contribute to chronic inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate allergic reactions.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing skin irritations and reducing the severity of allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers weighing exactly 250 grams.
Combine the dried flowers with 500 milliliters of organic sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a stainless steel pot.
Heat the mixture over low heat, whisking constantly, for 15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and let it cool for at least two hours.
Once cooled, transfer the cream to an airtight container, storing it in the refrigerator for up to six months or until ready to use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with allergies because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that soothe and calm irritated skin. The extracts from the maidenhair tree, specifically its leaves and stems, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling caused by allergic reactions. Additionally, the creams' emollient properties moisturize and hydrate the skin, helping to neutralize histamine release and alleviate symptoms such as itching, burning, and hives. This natural approach provides effective relief for individuals suffering from allergies without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maidenhair Tree cream contains flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and itching caused by allergic reactions.

Antihistamine properties: The cream's bioactive compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, exhibit antihistamine activity, blocking histamine release and reducing symptoms like itchiness, redness, and sneezing associated with allergies.

Soothing and calming effects: Maidenhair Tree contains a high concentration of glycosides, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, helping to reduce irritation, redness, and discomfort caused by allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing allergy symptoms such as itching and swelling.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help reduce histamine release, providing relief from allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from healthy plants and rinse thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves using a food dehydrator set at low heat for 6-8 hours.
Grind 30g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of coconut oil, 5g of beeswax, and 20g of shea butter with the powdered ginkgo biloba in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30-40 minutes, stirring frequently, until it reaches a temperature of 180°f to 190°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with allergies because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. The herb, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, contains compounds that work to calm down allergic reactions in the body. When applied topically as a cream, feverfew has been shown to relieve itching, redness, and swelling associated with allergic conditions such as eczema, hives, and insect bites. Additionally, its natural antihistamine-like effects help to block the release of histamines, which can further exacerbate allergy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Feverfew cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with allergic reactions, making it a natural relief for skin irritations.

Antihistamine actions: The bioactive compounds in feverfew cream, such as parthenolide, have antihistamine-like effects, which can help block the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic responses, and alleviate symptoms like itching and hives.

Immunomodulatory activity: Feverfew cream's immunomodulatory properties help regulate the immune system, reducing the overactive response to allergens and mitigating the severity of allergic reactions, making it a natural way to desensitize the body to allergenic substances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe skin irritations caused by allergies.
Leaves: Feverfew leaves are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help to reduce the risk of allergic reactions and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Heat 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler until it reaches a temperature of 65°c for 5 minutes.
Add the ground tanacetum parthenium powder to the heated coconut oil and stir for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 24 hours before straining and discarding the solids for 30 minutes.
Mix the resulting herbal infused oil with beeswax in a 1:1 ratio and heat until fully melted and combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with allergies because they contain the natural essence of valerian root, a plant-based ingredient that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, it reduces inflammation and itching associated with allergic reactions, providing quick relief from symptoms such as redness, swelling, and hives. Additionally, valerian's calming effect can soothe an irritated skin, promoting relaxation and reducing stress triggers that often exacerbate allergy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as valerenic acid, help reduce swelling and redness associated with allergic reactions.

Antihistamine effects: The alkaloids present in valerian, like isovaleric acid, mimic the action of antihistamines, blocking histamine receptors and reducing allergic symptoms like itching, sneezing, and congestion.

Calming and sedative properties: Valerian's ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety can help alleviate stress-related allergies, such as asthma, and provide relief from insomnia caused by allergy-related discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are used due to their high concentration of valepotriates, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can help soothe allergic reactions.
Rhizomes: The rhizome's rich content of valeric acid, an essential oil with anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, makes it a popular choice for medicinal creams to alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them in a warm oven at 50°c for two hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the ground root powder with four parts of coconut oil or sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring constantly for 30 minutes to create a smooth and consistent cream.
Remove the cream from heat and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it to sterilized jars and refrigerating at 5°c for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley creams helps with allergies because they contain flavonoids, antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, these compounds can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with allergic reactions. The cream's soothing effects can also calm redness and swelling, providing quick relief from itchy skin caused by environmental allergens like pollen or pet dander. Additionally, parsley's natural antihistamine properties can help alleviate symptoms of allergies, making it an effective and natural remedy for those who suffer from seasonal allergic reactions.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Parsley cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by allergic reactions.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in parsley cream, such as apigenin, reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with allergic skin conditions.
Antihistamine properties: Parsley's antihistamine-like effects help block histamine release, alleviating symptoms like itching, hives, and digestive issues caused by allergies.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm allergic reactions.
Seeds: Contain a compound called apiol, which has natural antihistamine effects and can help alleviate itching and redness associated with allergies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves from your garden or local market in the morning.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air dry them slowly.
Combine 20g of dried petroselinum crispum leaves with 200ml of boiling distilled water in a saucepan.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until fully melted.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool slightly, then pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with allergies because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with allergic reactions. The cream's soothing properties calm irritated skin, while its antioxidant-rich formula fights off free radicals that exacerbate allergic responses. Additionally, dandelion creams often combine with other herbs like chamomile and calendula to enhance their allergy-relieving effects, providing comprehensive relief from annoying symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with allergic reactions, providing relief to skin irritations.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate allergic responses, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting faster healing.
Immune system support: Dandelion's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune system, preventing excessive or imbalanced responses to allergens and reducing the risk of allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in inulin, a natural prebiotic that helps regulate gut health and reduce inflammation associated with allergic reactions.
Fruits: The fruits contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm skin irritation caused by allergies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after dew has dried for best potency retention.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale plants using shade drying or low-temperature dehydration within 24 hours of collection.
Combine one part dried taraxacum officinale with two parts sweet almond oil and blend for 10 minutes to create a herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with beeswax, coconut oil, and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 2:1:1 by weight, heating gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify before storing at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with allergies because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce histamine levels in the body. The cream's active compounds, such as polysaccharides and polyphenols, work to calm down allergic reactions, itching, and swelling. By doing so, it alleviates symptoms associated with pollen, pet dander, and other allergens. Additionally, stinging nettle creams may also help stabilize mast cells, which release histamine during an allergic response, further reducing the severity of allergic reactions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with allergic reactions.
Antihistamine activity: Stinging nettle creams exhibit antihistamine-like effects, blocking the action of histamine on its receptors in the body.
Immunomodulatory effects: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds that modulate the immune response, potentially helping to reduce allergic sensitization and improve tolerance to allergens.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Barks: Contains a high amount of silica, which helps to strengthen and repair damaged skin tissues, reducing allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather required herbs st. john's wort and echinacea in their pure forms dried or fresh.
Combine one part finely ground st. john's wort with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Mix 20 drops of echinacea tincture with the herb-oil mixture stirring well to create a uniform blend.
Heat the blend in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the heated mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar cooling it before use within two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The natural compounds in yarrow, such as chamazulene and apigenin, work to reduce swelling and itching caused by allergic reactions. When applied topically, the cream helps to calm irritated skin and mucous membranes, providing relief from symptoms like redness, hives, and congestion. By reducing inflammation and itching, yarrow creams can alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of calm and well-being for individuals with allergies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, help reduce swelling and itching associated with allergic reactions.

Antihistamine properties: Yarrow's antihistamine properties work by blocking the release of histamines, which are chemicals released during an allergic response, to alleviate symptoms like hives, itching, and redness.
Immune-modulating properties: Yarrow has been shown to stimulate the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off infections and reducing the body's overactive response to allergens.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like sesquiterpenes and polyacetylenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help reduce allergy symptoms.
Flowers: Containing bioactive flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-allergic activities, making them effective against allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves in the summer for optimal potency and flavor.
Combine 30 grams of harvested achillea millefolium leaves with 100 ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking occasionally, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix the strained herbal oil with 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 10 ml of distilled water in a double boiler, heating until smooth and consistent for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marigold creams helps with allergies because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herbal extract of marigold reduces swelling and redness associated with allergic reactions. By blocking histamine release and neutralizing free radicals, marigold creams provide quick relief from itchy skin, hives, and rashes, offering a natural and effective remedy for those suffering from seasonal or environmental allergies. This makes marigold creams a popular choice for allergy sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marigold creams help reduce inflammation caused by allergic reactions, such as redness, swelling, and itching.

Antihistamine: Marigold creams contain compounds that block the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for triggering allergic symptoms, thereby providing relief from itching, sneezing, and runny nose.

Antioxidant: Marigold creams contain antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate allergic reactions and promote skin conditions like eczema and atopic dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marigold roots are rich in antihistamine compounds that help to reduce itching and inflammation associated with allergies.
Leaves: Marigold leaves contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antiallergic properties, making them effective in soothing skin irritations.
Seeds: Marigold seeds are a good source of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a key player in allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh tagetes erecta flowers on a sunny morning when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or until they reach 10-12% moisture content.
Steep 20 grams of dried tagetes erecta flowers in 200ml of sweet almond oil for 4-6 hours to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the oil to a clean glass container.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth cream.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for allergies are topical creams or ointments that utilize plant extracts to alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as itching, redness, and swelling. These creams work by soothing the skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing. The best herbal creams for allergies include Calendula officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Symphytum officinale, Urtica dioica, Euphorbia peplus, Eucalyptus globulus, Plantago lanceolata, Melaleuca alternifolia, and Aloe vera. If left untreated, allergies can severely impact daily life, causing sleepless nights, missed work or school, and social isolation, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to allergies that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations, itchiness, and inflammation. Herbal creams can provide relief from conditions such as eczema, acne, rosacea, and dermatitis by soothing the skin and reducing redness and swelling. Herbs like aloe vera, calendula, and chamomile are commonly used in these creams due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help calm the skin and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with allergies is a blend of Aloe vera, Calendula, and Echinacea. Aloe vera soothes and calms irritated skin, while Calendula accelerates wound healing and reduces inflammation. Echinacea, on the other hand, boosts the immune system, fighting off underlying allergy triggers. When applied topically, these creams can provide relief from itchy skin, redness, and swelling. Regular use can also help desensitize the skin to allergens, reducing the severity of allergic reactions over time.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream soothes itchy and irritated skin, reducing the urge to scratch, which in turn can help to prevent the release of histamine and other allergic chemicals. By reducing inflammation and itching, Calendula officinalis creams provide relief from allergy symptoms such as redness, swelling, and discomfort. This natural remedy promotes skin health and can be an effective alternative to traditional allergy treatments.
remedy_constituents: Triterpene saponins: These saponins help reduce inflammation and prevent histamine release from mast cells, which contributes to allergic reactions.

Flavonoids (especially quercetin and kaempferol): These flavonoids have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of allergies by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Phenolic acids (especially caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These phenolic acids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, triterpenoids, and saponins, which provide anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: Leaves are also used because they contain similar compounds to flowers, offering additional anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits for soothing allergic skin irritations.
Stems: Stems are utilized due to their content of volatile oils, which contribute to the overall anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of calendula creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as jojoba oil in a 1:5 ratio for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Melt a combination of shea butter and beeswax in a double boiler at 120-140°f for 10-15 minutes.
Combine the infused oil with the melted shea butter and beeswax mixture in a 1:1 ratio.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk until it thickens and cools to a consistency suitable for application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with allergies because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream works by reducing inflammation and the release of histamine, a chemical that causes allergic reactions. Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects also help to boost the body's natural defense mechanisms, allowing the skin to recover from allergic attacks more efficiently. This natural remedy provides relief from redness, itching, and swelling associated with allergic skin conditions, promoting a healthier and more resilient skin barrier.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a key contributor to allergic reactions and symptoms.
Polyphenols: Acting as antioxidants, polyphenols help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate allergic responses, promoting overall immune system balance.
Alkylamides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as itching and congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe allergic reactions.
Roots: Contain alkaloids and glycosides that exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Leaves: Have been traditionally used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with allergies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a trusted source at the peak of their potency.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler to melt at 180-190°f in 10 minutes.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and add 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil.
Stir in 2 cups of distilled water and 1 teaspoon of glycerin to create a smooth and consistent texture in 5 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with allergies because they possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm irritated skin. The gel extracted from Aloe vera plants contains flavonoids, vitamins, and minerals that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with allergic reactions. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Aloe vera prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can exacerbate allergic symptoms. This natural remedy provides relief from itching, stinging, and burning sensations, making it an effective treatment for allergic skin conditions.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and itching associated with allergic reactions by modulating the immune system's response.
Fatty acids (specifically, linoleic and oleic acids): These fatty acids help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the symptoms of allergic reactions such as redness and swelling.
Polysaccharides (specifically, arabinose and galactose): These sugars help stabilize the skin's barrier function, preventing allergens from penetrating and triggering an immune response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloe-emodin and aloin.
Stems: The stems contain a significant amount of gel-like substance that can be extracted and used in creams to provide hydration and relief from allergic reactions.
Gel from Leaves: The gel is the clear, jelly-like substance found inside the leaves, and it's rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients that help to soothe and calm allergic skin.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of aloe barbadensis gel and transfer it to a blender to puree.
Mix 10g of vegetable emulsifier and 5g of vitamin e oil in a separate bowl.
Combine the blended aloe gel with the emulsifier mixture and blend for 2 minutes.
Heat 20g of distilled water to 70c in a double boiler and add 10g of beeswax.
Gradually add the heated mixture to the aloe gel mixture and blend until thickened.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in the plant, such as allantoin and mucilages, work together to reduce swelling and calm irritated skin. By doing so, it helps to alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, including redness, itching, and inflammation. This natural remedy provides relief from allergic conditions, making it an effective and holistic approach to managing allergies and promoting overall skin health. Regular use can lead to improved comfort and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce the symptoms of allergic reactions such as itching, redness, and swelling.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce the release of histamine and other mediators of allergic reactions, thereby alleviating symptoms.
Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to regulate the immune response and reduce the severity of allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to soothe and calm skin irritations.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties to reduce allergic reactions and itching.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system's response to allergens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried symphytum officinale root and 20g of dried symphytum officinale leaf in late summer.
Infuse the dried symphytum officinale root and leaf in 200ml of hot water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl discard solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add the infused symphytum officinale liquid to the beeswax mixture and stir until combined at 60°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with allergies because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as 5-oxo-levulinic acid and uric acid, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with allergic reactions. By soothing and calming the skin, Urtica dioica creams provide relief from common allergy symptoms, making them a natural and effective solution for managing seasonal and environmental allergies.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid with potent anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce the release of histamine, a key mediator of allergic reactions.
Isorhapontigenin: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that helps to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation associated with allergic responses.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with strong anti-inflammatory and anti-histamine properties that helps to inhibit the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reduce the severity of allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part, as they contain high concentrations of histamine-reducing compounds and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems are another popular choice, as they contain similar compounds to leaves and are often used in combination with leaves to enhance their effectiveness.
Roots: Roots of Urtica dioica are used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract the herb by steeping 20-30 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves in 100ml of hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container to remove solids.
Mix the liquid extract with 100-200 grams of petroleum jelly or beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes or until the mixture thickens.
Pour the thickened mixture into a clean container, let it cool, and store in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus creams helps with allergies because they contain 12-o-tetradecanoylphorbol-13-acetate (TPA), a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. This helps to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with allergic reactions. TPA in Euphorbia peplus creams also helps to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for triggering allergic symptoms. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving allergy symptoms. Its properties help to provide relief from allergies naturally.
remedy_constituents: Furanosterols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions such as skin redness and swelling.
Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals that contribute to allergic reactions and inflammation in the body.

Terpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, which help reduce the severity of allergic reactions by inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that contribute to allergic symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Seeds: Used to make creams for allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, as well as their ability to soothe skin irritations.
Stems: Used to make creams for allergies due to their anti-inflammatory properties and their ability to reduce swelling and redness.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20-30 grams of fresh euphorbia peplus leaves and stems using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin within 30 minutes.
Mix the extracted plant material with 100 grams of vegetable oil in a double boiler at low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container within 15 minutes.
Combine the strained oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency within 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with allergies because they contain the essential oil of Eucalyptus, which has natural decongestant properties. This oil helps to break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages and airways, making it easier to breathe. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Eucalyptus globulus also reduce swelling and itching associated with allergic reactions. By applying the cream topically, the oil is absorbed into the skin, providing quick relief from allergy symptoms such as congestion, itchiness, and redness.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Helps alleviate allergy symptoms by acting as a decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses.
Cineole: Exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with allergies.
Quercetin: Acts as an antihistamine, reducing the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions, and also has anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm allergic responses.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves are used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus barks are used due to their rich content of tannins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Essential oil from Leaves: Eucalyptus essential oil from leaves is used due to its decongestant and expectorant properties, which help relieve congestion and cough associated with allergies.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 1 liter of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Combine 100 grams of shea butter with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the infused oil to the shea butter and beeswax mixture and heat until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into a mold and let set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata creams helps with allergies because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and itching. The herb's leaves have been traditionally used to treat various respiratory issues, including congestion and coughs associated with allergies. The creams also contain antihistamines that neutralize histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions, which helps to alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and skin irritation. This natural remedy can provide relief for those suffering from seasonal and environmental allergies.
remedy_constituents: Apolipids: Help with allergies by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.
Phenolic glycosides (specifically aucubin and caffeic acid): Help with allergies by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which in turn reduce the severity of allergic reactions.
Flavonoids (specifically quercetin): Help with allergies by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce skin irritation and soothe allergic reactions.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, they aid in protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals.
Leaves: Their mucilaginous properties help to calm and moisturize the skin, providing relief from allergic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers for their medicinal properties and add them to a clean bowl.
Steam distill the harvested plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers in a stainless steel distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Combine 250ml of distilled water with 10g of the obtained essential oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes with occasional stirring until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with allergies because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredient, tea tree oil, has natural decongestant and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce swelling and ease congestion. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial and viral infections, which can exacerbate allergic reactions. By soothing and calming the skin, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can provide relief from itchy, red, and inflamed skin, making it an effective natural remedy for allergy sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps with allergies by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system's response to allergens.
Linalool: It helps with allergies by acting as an antihistamine, reducing the release of histamine that causes allergic reactions.
Terpineol: It helps with allergies by having anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergic reactions such as itching and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Used for their ability to provide a stable and long-lasting source of essential oils, which can help soothe and calm allergic skin irritations.
Barks: Used for their rich content of flavonoids and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of allergies.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 part of melaleuca alternifolia leaves in 2 parts of carrier oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter with 50 grams of the infused oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes or until the beeswax and shea butter melt.
Pour the melted mixture into a mold and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which work together to calm and moisturize the skin. Aloe vera also contains compounds that have natural antihistamine effects, which can help to reduce itching and redness caused by allergic reactions. By applying aloe vera cream to affected areas, individuals can find relief from symptoms of allergic reactions, promoting comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Bradykinin: It helps reduce histamine release, which is a key factor in allergic reactions, thereby alleviating symptoms like itching and inflammation.
Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps in reducing the allergic response and alleviating symptoms like redness and swelling.
Cinnamic acid: It acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps in reducing the allergic response and alleviating symptoms like itching and skin irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most widely used part due to its high gel content, which provides soothing and moisturizing properties.
Gel from Leaves: Specifically, the clear inner gel from the leaves is used as it has anti-inflammatory and calming effects that help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Extract from Leaves: This extract is derived from the leaves and is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with allergies.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 1/4 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in the double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Whisk the mixture for 5 minutes until it thickens and emulsifies completely.
Pour the herbal aloe vera cream into a glass jar and let it cool for 30 minutes.
